metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5344
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2c: USP14 is present in synaptoneurosome biochemical fractions prepared from mouse brain.

"To determine whether axJ/axJ mice also have defects in synaptic transmission in the CNS, we examined synaptic plasticity in the CA1-CA3 circuit of the hippocampus. Changes in short-term synaptic plasticity measured by paired pulse facilitation (PPF) were observed in axJ/axJ mice (Fig. 6b).

PPF occurs when two stimuli are delivered in rapid succession, resulting in a facilitation of the response to the second stimulus21. The level of PPF in axJ/axJ mice was lower than in the wildtype controls at short interpulse intervals, which indicates altered presynaptic calcium handling or altered sensitivity of the neurotransmitter-release machinery21.

Because there was no difference between the baseline synaptic transmission in axJ/axJ and wildtype mice (Fig. 6a), the defect in PPF may be due to changes in calcium handling rather than in the release machinery."

Together, presynaptic localization of USP14 is inferred.. Experimental description: M&M: "We obtained inbred C57BL/6J-axJ mice from The Jackson Laboratory (Bar Harbor, Maine, USA) to set up an intersubspecific cross with CAST/Ei mice ((C57BL/6J-axJ axJ × CAST/Ei)F1 × (C57BL/6J-axJ axJ × CAST/Ei)F1). "

antibody @ Acknowledgements:
"We thank ... A. Borodovsky and H. Ploegh for providing the antibodies against Usp14"
